observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees
one month before the checks become stale. Stale checks shall be reported
and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting
entries for unrecorded interest earned of P1,970.90, cancelled checks
amounting P70,424.23, and stale checks over 6 months but not over 2
years amounting to P95,332.23:
 For unrecored interest income
DR: Cash in Bank – LCCA P1,970.90
CR: Prior Year’s Adjustment P1,970.90
(*to represent surplus adjustment)
 For cancelled checks
DR: Cash in Bank – LCCA P70,424.23
CR: Prior Year’s Adjustment P70,424.23
 For stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years:
DR: Cash in Bank-LCCA P95,332.23
CR: Accounts Payable P95,332.23
c. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the schedule of
unreleased/unclaimed checks to the Municipal Accountant for the
preparation of the necessary JEV, to restore the amount equivalent to the
unclaimed checks to the CIB account and likewise to recognize the proper
payable/ liability account. The following entry should be used:
 Entry to record unclaimed checks at year-end:
DR: Cash in Bank account xx
CR: Appropriate Payable/ Liability account xx
 Entry at issuing year to reverse the above entry
DR: Appropriate Payable/ Liability account xx
CR: Cash in Bank account xx
d. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the
authorized depository bank to confirm the existence of bank accounts
totaling P298,588.83 and require the Municipal Accountant to prepare
bank reconciliation statement thereof.
e. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile
their records monthly to provide accurate balances in the cash in bank
and in order to make necessary adjustments for discrepancies that may
be noted.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Require the Inventory Team to complete the physical count of all
properties as at year-end and periodically conduct physical count of
PPEs which should be done annually and in the RPCPPE provide details
on the balance per card and balance per count;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer-Designate to exert
effort to determine the composition of PPE adjustments recorded in lump
amount.
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Supply Officer
to locate the missing PPEs costing P3,213,663.90 and submit proof of its
existence to COA.
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the depreciation expense
amounting to P212,813.72 for current year’s procurements of PPE.
e. Require the Supply Officer-Designate to maintain property cards and
reconciled with the ledgers cards of the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record every January of
each year the full amount of RPT due and collectible as receivables based on
the computed amount per RPT Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index
cards which is also based on the Assessment Roll from the Municipal Assessor

We also recommend that the Municipal Assessor:
a) Conduct physical surveys to verify and determine whether all real
properties within the Municipality are properly listed in the assessment
rolls;
b) Undertake appraisal and general revision of real property assessments every
three (3) years as required in Section 219 of RA 7160 in order to come up
with a reliable amount of assessment on real property taxes;
c) Update and reconcile all records pertaining to real properties within the
Municipality and its owners.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) Update the Taxpayer’s Index Card and reconcile the same with the records of
the Municipal Assessors;
b) Submit the Taxpayer’s Index Card to the Municipal Accountant at the start of
each year as basis in recording RPT Receivables;
c) Actively implement tax collection methods and strategies to increase RPT
collections to prevent bloating the balances of uncollected RPT in the
books;
d) Prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained
uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year including reasons for such non-
collection, and;
e) Submit the Certified list of Real Property Tax Delinquencies to the
Sangguniang Bayan (SB) on or before December 31 of the year
immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, and request for
assistance from the SB in the enforcement of the remedies for collection
provided in RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all Revenue Collection
Clerks to remit to the Municipal Treasurer the full amount of their
collections on a daily basis regardless of the nature of collections made. Also,
it is highly recommended to transfer the depository accounts to LBP San
Juan Branch, which is nearer to the LGU.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit all her
collections, as well as collections turned over to her by the Revenue
Collecting Clerks, daily or not later than the next banking day.

We also recommended to the Municipal Mayor through the BAC:
a. To execute contracts within (10) calendars after the receipt of the Notice
of Award.
b. To submit justification why the Municipality executed the contracts
enumerated in Table 3 prior to the receipt of Notice of Award by the
Contractor.
c. To issue the Notice to Proceed within (7) calendar days from the
execution of the contract.
d. To impose the liquidated damages for the delay in the completion of the
projects enumerated in Table 17.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC to submit
written justification why award of contract was made to the two contractors
despite having poor performance in their on-going contracts as disclosed in
the “Statement of All On-going Government and Private Contracts Including
Contracts Awarded But Not Yet Started”.

We also recommended to the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC to submit
written justification why the latter was able to recommend an award of
contract despite the omission of necessary data in the “Statement of All On-
going Government and Private Contracts Including Contracts Awarded But
Not Yet Started”.

Lastly, for future procurements of infrastructure projects we recommended
to the BAC to thoroughly scrutinize the post-qualification of the bidding
documents and to require, where necessary, documents to thoroughly
undertake the post-qualification of the bidders.

We commended the Municipal Mayor and the concerned officials and
employees for the optimal implementation of the PPAs under the CY 2021
20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the MDRRMC to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP)
indicating the (i) detailed PPAs and the corresponding appropriation
classified as either Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE)
or Capital Outlay (CO); and (ii) under a separate caption, the list of
projects and activities charges to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous
years for ease of monitoring of project implementation and evaluation.
The prescribed sample format of the LDRRMFIP is provided below.

b) MDRRMC ensure that the LDRRMF be strictly utilized for the
implementation of projects and activities for disaster prevention and
mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery and
allowable COVID-19 related expenses;
c) Require the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan to enact a supplemental
budget for any changes in the Annual Budget to cover new PPAs from
realigned funds or new available funds from new revenue source as
certified by the Municipal Treasurer.
d) Require the members of the Local Development Council and Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to prepare a
supplemental AIP to support the changes in the Annual budget.
e) Ensure the refund of honoraria received by the frontliners during the
conduct Pfizer vaccination without prejudice for the issuance of Notice of
Disallowance.
f) Municipal Accountant to (i) comply with the monthly submission of the
Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF; (ii) and maintain
corresponding Subsidiary Ledger for each type of Disaster Response and
Recue Equipment.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Direct the MDRRMO, MHO-Designate, and other department in-charge
of the isolation facility to submit the Attendance Sheet of frontliners on
duty and the submit a certification detailing the names of quarantined
PUIs, PUMs, and Covid-19 patients duly certified by the Municipal
Health Officer with details on date of arrival and release from the facility.
b. Direct the MHO to submit the complete list of attendance during the
conduct of vaccination program of the Municipality.
c. Direct the MDRRMO to submit the complete list of participants on the
various disaster preparedness trainings conducted by the Municipality.
d. Direct the MDRRMO and DSDWD to submit the distribution list of 190
sacks of rice with details on the quantity received by each beneficiary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Rural Health Unit personnel and other concerned offices to submit
lacking supporting documents/ records/ orders to the Audit Team for
payment of SRA and AHDP and accordingly observe compliance on the
documentary requirements for these kind of personnel incentives to avoid
suspension in audit;
b. Municipal Health Officer to submit a written justification to allow payment of
Hazard Pay on days that involved performance of activities that does not
directly cater to prevention, detection, isolation, and treatment of COVID-19
towards the goal of reducing the number of cases and death, as well as the
payment of Hazard Pay to eight (8) BHWs that were not duly-accredited by
the Rural Health Unit of the Municipality, without prejudice on the issuance
of Notice of Disallowance on unwarranted claims.
c. Municipal Accountant to ensure all necessary supporting documents are
attached to claims before affixing his certification in the DV; and
d. Municipal Accountant to follow the proper recording of transactions to
achieve faithful presentation of accounts in the financial statements,
pursuant to IPSAS I.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to exercise due diligence in the processing of claims and complete documentations must be observed before effecting payment.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the subsidiary accounts with negative balances on the period when an over-remittance is made and effect the necessary adjustment to correct the discrepancies and thus present correct balances of Due to GSIS and Due to PAG-IBIG in the financial statements.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to fully utilize all the available resources in developing GAD Database in order to come up with a more sex-disaggregated data that is in consonance with the provisions stated above.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Human Resource Management Officer to adhere to the guidelines set forth in CSC Memorandum Circular No. 17 s. 2002. Further, job order workers shall be charged against local projects authorized by the local sanggunian and not against the MOOE of any office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the local sanggunian in coordination with the national government to do some fundamental jobs, such as: a) Crafting ordinances and issuing Executive Orders related to waste environmental protection; b) Perfecting the system of current waste management and Monthly BRS; c) Adding more waste recycling facilities; d) Focusing on the recycling methods of the recyclables and Landfill management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks.
Furthermore, require the Municipal Treasurer to follow the procedures stated on Section 59 of NGAS for Spoiled and Stale Checks, as follows:
a. For spoiled checks which are immediately cancelled and for which the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) has not yet been prepared, the cancelled check shall be attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued and the word “Cancelled” shall be indicated on the report.
b. For stale checks which have been unclaimed and thus, the original DV and supporting documents are still with the Treasurer, the cancelled check shall be presented in the RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated in the report. The original DV and supporting documents shall be returned to the Accountant who shall prepare a JEV to record the transaction as Accounts Payable.
c. For checks which became spoiled or stale in the hands of the payee and which require replacement, a new check may be issued upon submission of the spoiled or stale check to the Treasurer. A certified copy of the DV shall be requested from the Auditor for presentation to the Administrator/Municipal Mayor who shall countersign the check. The cancelled check shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation. The replacement check shall also be reported chronologically in the RCI.

Checks that are
still outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
1.19. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Inventory Team to conduct the annual physical inventory of
PPE by type and submit a Report on the Physicial Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE) not later than January 31 of each year and
reconcile the RPCPPE with the ledger balances;
b. Require the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards and
reconcile them with the ledger cards of the Municipal Accounting Office;
and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) recognize depreciation expense
monthly on all the PPE accounts; (b) make necessary adjustments in books
to correct unrecognized deprecation of identified three PPE accounts and
make necessary disclosure in the Notes to FS as to the acquisition cost,
additions, deductions and Accumulated Depreciation of PPE accounts; and
(c) adopt all the principles prescribed in IPSAS 17 for proper accounting of
the PPE so as to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

2.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to make necessary corrections in her cash-
in treasury cashbook and also the Municipal Accountant should do the
same in the general ledger;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the guidelines
provided in the submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS)
by submitting monthly. Make necessary journal entries to record book
reconciling items in the books of accounts with attachments of necessary
documents to support the reconciling items enumerated in the BRS;
c. Direct Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to payees one month
before the checks become stale. Stale checks shall be reported and
attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation; and
d. The Municipal Accountant should make the necessary adjusting entries for
stale checks as follows:

Particulars Account Title Account Debit Credit
Code
Lost/Destroyed/Stale/Obsolete
Checks
Checks issued in the current/prior year for replacement
Check Cancellation Cash-in-Bank, LCCA 1-01-02-010 xx
Accounts Payable 2-01-01-010 xx
Replacement Accounts Payable 2-01-01-010 xx
Cash in Bank, LCCA 1-01-02-010 xx

3.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Strictly require each Department Head to comply with the monitoring and
reporting of inventories issued and prepare the SSMI on a monthly basis
and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV based on SSMIs and RIS
to record as expenses the issued/used inventories;
c. Constitute an Inventory Committee which shall conduct the physical
count of inventories and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January
31 each year;
d. Require the RHU to prepare Monthly Report of Inventory of Drugs and
Medicines and Medical Supplies; and
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal voucher to record back
to inventory the unissued medicines.

4.11 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to classify and record all the procured
supplies and materials from LDRRMF to the appropriate inventory
account;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and
Supplies Ledger Cards for all PPE and relief goods in compliance to
LGUs New Government Accounting System (NGAS) specifically on the
provisions pertaining to the recording of deliveries of supplies or property in
the books of accounts and the reporting on issuance of supplies/materials of
procured items funded out from the LDRRMF;
c. Require the Supply officer, as the overall Property Officer to maintain
Property Cards and Stock Cards for monitoring purposes of the
consumption and balances of the inventory of supplies; and
d. Require the Property Officer to issue RSI for all issuances of inventory
and prepare and submit the RSMI together with the RSI to the Municipal
Accountant monthly as basis in recording the expenses in the books of
accounts.

6.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Local Development Council to prepare a Supplemental
Investment Program and make representation with Sanggunian to enact a
Supplemental Budget to support the BGCM expenditures;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to classify and record all the procured
supplies and materials charged under BGCM to the appropriate inventory
account;
c. Require also the Supply Officer, as the overall Property Officer to
maintain Stock Cards for monitoring purposes of the consumption and
balances of the inventory of supplies; and
d. Direct the Municipal Property and Supply Unit Officer to prepare and
submit the RSMI to the Municipal Accountant monthly as basis in
recording the expenses in the books of accounts.

7.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to enact a Supplemental
Budget for COVID 19 related programs/projects/activities in order to
comply with the provisions of Section 321 of RA 7160 and Section 2.3 of
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01; and
b. Direct the Local Development Council to prepare a Supplemental
Investment Program in order to have a basis in the enactment of
Supplemental Budget for COVID related expenditures.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor:
1.8.1 to direct the General Services Officer-Designate to conduct a complete
inventory taking of all the assets of the Municipality and prepare a
complete and properly filled-up RPCPPE which shall be submitted to
the Audit Team as required by regulations.
1.8.2 to require the Accounting and Property Divisions to reconcile their
respective records regularly and maintain PPELC and PC,
respectively, to facilitate reconciliation of PPE book balance with the
physical inventory report balance.

2.9 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to regularly prepare the BRS for the reconciliation of the cash in bank
balances and accounting records and effect the necessary adjustments,
where appropriate. For the bank reconciling items, the Municipal Accountant
should make representations with the depository bank for the necessary
adjustments.

3.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to draw journal entry vouchers to adjust the balance of the loans payable
account and to reclassify loan amortizations due for the next twelve months
from balance sheet date, on all long term-debts, to current liabilities.

4.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to a) Identify and trace the causes of the unreconciled balances of the
reciprocal accounts; and b) Maintain and update regularly the subsidiary
ledgers of the said accounts and reconcile with the balances in the general
ledger.

5.6 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks. Furthermore,
require the Municipal Treasurer to follow the procedures stated on Section 59
of NGAS for LGUS for Spoiled and Stale Checks, as follows:
a. For spoiled checks which are immediately cancelled and for which the
Report of Checks Issued (RCI) has not yet been prepared, the cancelled
check shall be attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the
other checks issued and the word “Cancelled” shall be indicated on the
report.
b. For stale checks which have been unclaimed and thus, the original DV and
supporting documents are still with the Treasurer, the cancelled check shall
be presented in the RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated
in the report. The original DV and supporting documents shall be returned to
the Accountant who shall prepare a JEV to record the transaction as
Accounts Payable.
c. For checks which became spoiled or stale in the hands of the payee and
which require replacement, a new check may be issued upon submission of
the spoiled or stale check to the Treasurer. A certified copy of the DV shall
be requested from the Auditor for presentation to the
Administrator/Municipal Mayor who shall countersign the check. The
cancelled check shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the
period of cancellation. The replacement check shall also be reported
chronologically in the RCI.

6.5 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare an individual Subsidiary Ledger for each account appearing in the
General Ledger to facilitate the tracing of specific transaction posted therein.

7.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the management to
prepare and submit a complete and properly prepared Report on Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as required in Section 124 of the
Manual on the NGAs for LGUs, Volume I.

8.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to exercise due diligence in the processing of claims and complete
documentations must be observed before effecting payment.

9.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Assessor to
expedite the processing of land titles in favor of the Municipality. We also
recommended that no improvements be made on the land unless proof of
ownership is established by a certificate of title.

10.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to (a) direct the Municipal
Treasurer to submit a Status Report on the Revenue Code; and (b)
coordinate with the members of the Sangguniang Bayan to cause the
updating of the Revenue Code based on the report of the Municipal
Treasurer.

11.10 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, being the head of the Municipal
Development Council to strictly require the preparation of the Annual
Investment Plan which should contain a detailed information of the specific
program, project and activities and which should partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures.

12.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Council to prepare the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan as a basis for the release and
use of the fund.

13.9 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to adapt monitoring mechanism to detect variances in the amount to be
remitted and withheld. Furthermore, we also recommend that accounting
records such as cash receipts journal, check disbursement journal, cash
disbursement journal, general and subsidiary ledgers must be updated
monthly in order to determine the exact amount to be remitted to the Bureau
of Internal Revenue.

14.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to trace the subsidiary accounts with negative balances on the period when
an over-remittance is made and effect the necessary adjustment to correct
the discrepancies and thus present correct balances of Due to GSIS and
Due to PAG-IBIG in the financial statements.

15.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to adapt monitoring mechanism to detect variances in the amount to be
remitted and withheld. Furthermore, we also recommended that accounting
records such as cash receipts journal, check disbursement journal, cash
disbursement journal, general and subsidiary ledgers must be updated
monthly in order to determine the exact amount to be remitted to the
PhilHealth.

16.5 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Social
Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) fully utilize all the available
resources in developing GAD Database in order to come up with a more sex-
disaggregated data that is in consonance with the provisions stated above.

17.12 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Human Resource
Management Officer to adhere to the guidelines set forth in CSC
Memorandum Circular No. 40 s. 1998. Further, job order workers shall be
charged against local projects authorized by the local sanggunian and not
against the MOOE of any office.

18.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and the local sanggunian to:
18.7.1 craft ordinances and issue executive orders related to waste
management and environmental protection;
18.7.2 perfect the current waste management system of the Municipality,
including, garbage classification, collection, and recycling;
18.7.3 add more waste recycling facilities; and
18.7.4 focus on the recycling methods of the recyclables.

19.9 We recommend to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant:
19.9.1 to exert all effort to locate the documents to support the
disallowances;
19.9.2 to send demand letters to those employees who have not yet settled
their disallowances; and,
19.9.3 to strictly monitor the settlement of suspension and disallowances
and communicate to the Audit Team the specific transaction on this
account that cannot be retrieved anymore.

20.8 We recommended to that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal
Accountant:
20.8.1 To locate all the source documents and MOA with the source
agencies to review the terms and conditions corresponding to the
funds transferred in relation to the implementation of programs and
projects;
20.8.2 To determine if the specific programs or projects for which the funds
were transferred were already implemented, goods/services were
already delivered, liquidation of funds were already submitted and
received by the source agencies, and liabilities were already settled
for proper adjustments in the books;
20.8.3 To return to the source agency any unused/unexpended balance if so
stipulated in the MOA; and
20.8.4 To request authority for the write off of dormant fund transfer
accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016, if warranted, duly supported by a quarterly aging
of dormant fund transfers and other relevant documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to undertake measures in managing and validating all recorded balances for PPE items per the agency’ books of accounts thru the regular conduct of physical count and maintenance of prescribed records by their respective offices. Moreover, specific courses of actions shall be carried out as follows, among others: a) In the absence of a GSO, a property custodian in the treasurer’s office to take custody of all properties and who will be responsible for the maintenance of PPE cards shall be designated; b) In the absence of a centralized stockroom for supplies, a property custodian for every office who shall be designated to take custody of all inventories and maintain stock cards; c) A committee who will conduct physical count regularly of all inventories as well as PPE items should be created; and d) The Municipal Accountant shall maintain a complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for inventories and PPE items. All records shall be updated and reconciled regularly with issuances of inventories and PPE items duly supported with Requisition Issue Slips for inventories and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Health Officer to follow strictly the rules and regulations on Philhealth Capitation in order to achieve the objectives in the creation of the funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the prescribed maintenance of special account for all loan proceeds for which financial reports shall be prepared.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the practice of checking the completeness of documentations before claims are to be paid. Said measure must be carried out to avoid suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit. Likewise, suppliers of goods and services must be required to submit required documentations as mandated under Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure that procured items are from legitimate sources.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Compel the Municipal Accountant to promptly and regularly prepare the required Report on Sources and Utilization which shall be subject to audit by COA. a. Direct the LDRRM Council to implement the planned program/projects and activities in order to achieve the objectives of LDRRM fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the chart of accounts of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in recording expenses using the correct account title and code in order to present a reliable and accurate financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the Annual Budget an appropriation equivalent to 1% of IRA for LCPC to adhere to the provisions of Section 15 of RA 9344 in order to fund LCPC activities. Furthermore, the Local Chief Executive should cause the creation of the Local Council for the Protection of Children and direct the said council to prepare Annual Work and Financial Plan for the attainment of the LCPC projects and activities for the welfare of the children.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to make reconciliation of its records in order to reflect the correct amount of cash balance in local treasury and install strong internal control measures in recording of cash in consonance with Section F of the LTOM.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the Annual Budget for 20% development fund in conformity with the JMC No. 17-1 of the DILG and DBM in order to avoid suspensions and/or disallowance in post audit.

Considering the LGU would continuously secure loans and borrowings to finance development and investment projects, the Audit Team recommended measures to address the deficiencies:  The maximum borrowing capacity should not only consider the 20% debt service ceiling but also the LGUs’ net available funds prior to availing funds for contracting of loans/borrowings; and  Subsidiary ledgers should be maintained for each loan/borrowing. On the other hand, the LGUs through the Local Executive should conduct self-assessment of their capacity to repay the loan taking into consideration their net available funds, ensure that utilization of loan proceeds is covered by appropriation ordinance, determine the most cost effective borrowing scheme, contract loans only after proper evaluation of priority projects, and allocate sufficient funds to cover total contracted liabilities.

For those officials adopting a work schedule other than usual, attach a certified list indicating percentage of sessions attended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended exercise prudence in the use of government funds specifically: a. Seminars/trainings where participants come from the province should be conducted within the province in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other relative projects. b. Send participants only to seminars with participants from their area of concern. c. Make representation with the Officers of the PCL to conform with the regulations on seminars/trainings as mandated by COA and DILG. d. Documentary requirements such as Activity Design, Post Activity Report and other necessary documents e.g. approval/authority from concerned government office for the conduct of seminar (DILG) should be attached once the participant will attend the seminar/training.
observations.reason: Projects and activities should be documented and participants limited to those from area of concern to minimize cost.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow COA Circular 96-011 by preparing and submitting the monthly bank reconciliation statement within the time frame provided by law and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The local government submitted the status of the Bank Reconciliation Statement as of the date of the statement and adjustments were made.
observations.reason: Submission of the BRS was beyond the required time frame; latest submission was made for the month of November 2016 and December 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly comply with the requirements on the submission of the perfected contracts and other supporting documents within five (5) days from execution pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 87-278 dated November 12, 1987, 89-299 dated March 21, 1989 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: See Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should review and analyze the unremitted balances of these trust liabilities and remit the same to the concerned agencies to avoid penalties/ interest, deprivation of employees’ privileges and/or misuse of the funds.

We recommended that the Collectors and Municipal Treasurer submit to the Audit Team a Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF), respectively, to ascertain the status of the accountable forms in their possession.
observations.reason: Management submitted only the RAAF and CRAAF upon demand by the Audit Team.
